title: Spironolactone and Hydrochlorothiazide: MedlinePlus Drug Information
text:
iarrhea loss of appetite stomach pain frequent urination dizziness headache enlarged or painful breasts irregular menstrual periods difficulty maintaining or achieving an erection vaginal bleeding in post-menopausal ('after the change of life', the end of monthly menstrual periods) women drowsiness Some side effects can be serious. If you experience any of these symptoms, call your doctor immediately: 
muscle weakness or cramps changes in vision or eye pain rapid, excessive weight loss fatigue slow or irregular heartbeat unusual bruising or bleeding yellowing of the skin or eyes skin rash itching fever confusion If you experience a serious side effect, you or your doctor may send a report to the Food and Drug Administration's (FDA) MedWatch Adverse Event Reporting program online ( https://www.fda.gov/Safety/MedWatch ) or by phone ( 1-800-332-1088 ).
What should I know about storage and disposal of this medication? 
Keep this medicine in the container it came in, tightly closed, and out of reach of children. Store it at room temperature and away from excess heat and moisture (not in the bathroom).
Dispose of unneeded medications in a way so that pets, children, and other people cannot take them. Do not flush this medication down the toilet. Use a medicine take-back program. Talk to your pharmacist about take-back programs in your community. Visit the FDA's Safe Disposal of Medic
